title: Trickle parameters four-way split: canonical 4096ms vs Rust 256ms vs C 1000ms vs advertised dio_int_min=3 (8ms)
description: Canonical: constants.toml:56-60 and spec/appendix-rpl.md:36 = imin 4096ms, doublings 8, k 10. Python constants.py:52-54 matches. Rust lichen-node/src/routing.rs:201,215: TrickleTimer::new(256, 8, 10) - 16x faster than canonical. C lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/Kconfig:40-54: default 1000ms. Worse: the on-wire advertised DODAG Configuration option defaults (Rust message.rs:235, C messages.c:183, vector opt_dodag_config_default) use dio_int_min=3 = 8ms - matching NONE of the runtimes nor the spec (spec-conformant would be dio_int_min=12). So every node advertises a Trickle regime no node runs, and the three runtimes disagree 16x apart. Complements et78.3.2 (Zephyr MUST checks) with the concrete numeric split. Fix: converge runtimes on 4096/8/10 (or redefine canonical), fix advertised dio_int_min in all three codecs + regenerate the vector.
